records.parent_question: Critical Products: Chemicals
records.vulnerability: The chemical most critical to facility core operations is not available from multiple suppliers. In the event that the provider is unable to provide this chemical, facility operations may be severely impacted.
records.ofc_text: Collaborate with the provider to become better informed about the company's business continuity management program, notification procedures for supply disruptions, and the overall resilience of the supply system. Use this information to improve the facility's own business continuity plans, policies, and procedures, as necessary.

records.parent_question: Critical Products: Chemicals
records.ofc_text: Evaluate the need to store this critical chemical onsite to mitigate the impacts of a disruption in deliveries from the primary provider. As part of the evaluation, consider potential requirements associated with storage of hazardous chemicals subject to Federal security regulations.

records.parent_question: Is external natural gas required for the Facility for Core Operations (Produce Key Services, Goods)?_x000D_
records.vulnerability: The facility has only one natural gas service connection, creating a potential single point of failure in its natural gas supply.
records.ofc_text: Collaborate with natural gas utility provider on the feasibility of installing a second service connection that enters the facility in a different location to create redundancy.

records.parent_question: Is external natural gas required for the Facility for Core Operations (Produce Key Services, Goods)?_x000D_
records.vulnerability: The natural gas service connections for the facility are not each capable of handling the entire facility load. If service from one connection was disrupted, the natural gas supply for the facility would be degraded.
records.ofc_text: Collaborate with the natural gas utility provider on the feasibility of modifying service connections such that any one connection could meet the entire facility load.

records.parent_question: Does the facility require external wastewater discharge services for Core Operations (Produce Key Services, Goods)?
records.vulnerability: The facility has only one service connection for the removal of wastewater from the site, creating a potential single point of failure. Disruption of service from this single pipeline could degrade facility operations.
records.ofc_text: Collaborate with the wastewater utility provider on the feasibility of installing a second service connection that enters the facility in a different location to create redundancy.

records.parent_question: Does the facility require external wastewater discharge services for Core Operations (Produce Key Services, Goods)?
records.vulnerability: The wastewater service connections for the facility are not each capable of handling the entire facility load. If service from one connection was disrupted, wastewater removal service for the facility would be degraded.
records.ofc_text: Collaborate with the wastewater utility provider on the feasibility of implementing infrastructure upgrades to mitigate potential impacts from the disruption of one of the wastewater service connections to the facility.

records.parent_question: Are you aware of the DHS "See Something Say Something" campaign?
records.child_node: No   (If No, PSA should provide flyer or information on program)
records.vulnerability: Facility personnel are not aware of the nationwide "If You See Something, Say Something™" public awareness campaign. This program is intended to raise public awareness of indicators of terrorism and terrorism-related crime and to emphasize the importance of reporting suspicious activity to the proper authorities.
records.ofc_text: Consult your local PSA for information about the "If You See Something, Say Something™" public awareness campaign. For more information, visit the DHS Website at http://www.dhs.gov/if-you-see-something-say-something%E2%84%A2-campaign.

records.parent_question: Are you aware of the DHS "See Something Say Something" campaign?
records.child_node: No   (If No, PSA should provide flyer or information on program)
records.ofc_text: Access video and audio public service announcements encouraging the public to contact local authorities if they see suspicious activity at http://www.dhs.gov/if-you-see-something-say-something-public-service-announcements.

records.parent_question: Are you aware of any of the following agencies with which you can exchange information?  (check all that apply)
records.vulnerability: The facility does not exchange information with the FBI. Information exchange with the FBI would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
records.ofc_text: Consult your local PSA regarding opportunities to enhance information sharing with the FBI. The FBI has a variety of major information sharing initiatives for sharing timely, relevant, and actionable intelligence. For more information, visit the FBI Website at http://www.fbi.gov/stats-services/publications/national-information-sharing-strategy-1.

records.parent_question: Are you aware of any of the following agencies with which you can exchange information?  (check all that apply)

records.parent_question: Are you aware of any of the following agencies with which you can exchange information?  (check all that apply)
records.ofc_text: The CDC’s PHIN is a national initiative to increase the capacity of public health agencies to electronically exchange data and information across organizations and jurisdictions (e.g., clinical care to public health, public health to public health and public health to other federal agencies). Contact PHIN by phone or email, at 770-454-4863 or PHINTECH@CDC.GOV, or visit the CDC Website for more information, at http://www.cdc.gov/phin/about/index.html.
